# Misheyakir

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Misheyakir 10 Point 2 Degrees

Misheyakir according to some opinions, based on the sun being 10.2° below geometric zenith.

Calculated for when the sun is 10.2° below geometric zenith, corresponding to the sun’s position 45 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/).

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir10Point2Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir10Point2Degrees`

## Misheyakir 11 Degrees

Misheyakir according to some opinions, based on the sun being 11° below geometric zenith.

Calculated for when the sun is 11° below geometric zenith, corresponding to the sun’s position 48 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/).

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir11Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir11Degrees`

## Misheyakir 11 Point 5 Degrees

Misheyakir according to some opinions, based on the sun being 11.5° below geometric zenith.

Calculated for when the sun is 11.5° below geometric zenith, corresponding to the sun’s position 52 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/).

The source notes that this calculation should be recalculated.

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir11Point5Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir11Point5Degrees`

## Misheyakir 12 Point 85 Degrees

This is deprecated because it is a very early misheyakir time that should only be used bish’as hadchak. Lechatchila, a later zman should be used.

A very early misheyakir time mentioned for use bish’as hadchak, based on the sun being 12.85° below geometric zenith.

Calculated for when the sun is 12.85° below geometric zenith. It is based on the sun’s position slightly later than 57 minutes before sunrise in Jerusalem [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/).

This zman is mentioned for use bish’as hadchak in the Birur Halacha [Tinyana](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=50535&st=&pgnum=88) and [Tlisa’ah](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=50537&st=&pgnum=31) in Orach Chaim siman 18 as 12.85°.

Actual calculations show the 57-minute time to be slightly more than 12.9°, while 12.85° is described as a slight chumra compared with 12.9°, a difference of about 14 seconds at the equinox/equilux in Jerusalem.

The 57-minute based time is mentioned by the Minchas Yitzchak [vol. 9, siman 9](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=1601&st=&pgnum=21) as 15 minutes before alos hashachar, though the location is not clear and no degree-based conversion is mentioned.

The Kaf Hachaim [vol. 1 siman 18, no. 18](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=8140&st=&pgnum=81) states that in Yerushalayim 60 fixed minutes are used year round. Calculations show that 60 fixed minutes in Yerushalayim ranges from 13.5° at the spring equinox to 11.5° at the summer solstice, while 57-minute misheyakir ranges from 12.9° at the winter equinox to 11° at the summer solstice.

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir12Point85Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir12Point85Degrees`

## Misheyakir 7 Point 65 Degrees

Misheyakir based on the sun being 7.65° below geometric zenith.

Calculated for when the sun is 7.65° below geometric zenith. The degree value is based on a 35/36-minute zman [around the equinox / equilux](https://kosherjava.com/2022/01/12/equinox-vs-equilux-zmanim-calculations/), when the neshef is shortest.

This time is based on [Rabbi Moshe Feinstein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein), who writes in [Ohr Hachaim Vol. 4, Ch. 6](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=14677&pgnum=7) that misheyakir in New York is 35–40 minutes before sunrise, described as a drop less than 8°.

[Rabbi Yisroel Taplin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yisroel_Taplin) in [Zmanei Yisrael](https://www.worldcat.org/oclc/889556744) notes that [Rabbi Yaakov Kamenetsky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yaakov_Kamenetsky) stated that it is not less than 36 minutes before sunrise, possibly 40 minutes.

This is described as the accepted [minhag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minhag) in [Lakewood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lakewood_Township,_New_Jersey) used in the [Yeshiva](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beth_Medrash_Govoha), following the opinion of [Rabbi Shmuel Kamenetsky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shmuel_Kamenetsky), who provided the time of 35/36 minutes but not a degree-based time.

Since this zman depends on the level of light, Rabbi Yaakov Shakow presented this degree-based calculation to Rabbi Shmuel Kamenetsky, who agreed to it.

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir7Point65Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir7Point65Degrees`

## Misheyakir 9 Point 5 Degrees

Misheyakir based on the sun being 9.5° below geometric zenith.

Calculated for when the sun is 9.5° below geometric zenith. This is based on [Rabbi Dovid Kronglass’s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dovid_Kronglas) calculation of 45 minutes in Baltimore, mentioned in [Divrei Chachamim No. 24](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=20287&pgnum=29) and brought in [Birur Halacha, Tinyana, Ch. 18](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=50535&pgnum=87).

Rabbi Yaakov Yitzchok Neiman is cited as saying that the Vya’an Yosef did not want to rely on times earlier than 45 minutes in New York.

This zman is also used in calendars published by Rabbi Hershel Edelstein and by Edot Hamizrach communities.

This also follows the opinion of [Rabbi Shmuel Kamenetsky](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shmuel_Kamenetsky), who provided times of 36 and 45 minutes but not degree-based times. Since this zman depends on the level of light, Rabbi Yaakov Shakow presented these degree-based times to Rabbi Shmuel Kamenetsky, who agreed to them.

This time may not be available in northern and southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not go low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir9Point5Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMisheyakir9Point5Degrees`
